_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to collect, check and display data, and describe distributions using mode and shape.

_source.w: We are learning to collect, check and display data, and describe distributions using mode and shape.
y: Describing the shape and mode of a dataset gives us a summary of what the data looks like without listing every value.
Checking data for errors before analysing it is a critical step that real statisticians always take.
These skills help us make sense of information in science projects, surveys and media reports.
eg: Collecting class data on shoe sizes and finding the mode (most common size).
Describing a distribution as symmetric, skewed left or skewed right.
Checking data for impossible values before creating a graph.
Creating a dot plot and describing the shape of the distribution.
Identifying outliers — values that seem very different from the rest.
Comparing two datasets by their modes and overall shapes.
